name: Seawall Promenade
description: A walkway along the top of Solverde's seawall where the whole town gathers at dusk. The place where community happens without anyone organizing it.

name: Morning Market
description: Stalls and tables along the harbor edge where the day's catch, baked goods, garden produce, and salvage change hands. Busiest at dawn, quiet by midday.

name: Cala's Bakery
tagline: The smell that wakes the town
description: A bakery on the edge of the morning market. Whitewashed walls, terracotta roof, flour dust in the pre-dawn light. Cala starts before anyone else is awake.

name: The Driftwood
description: Solverde's tavern, built from salvaged ship timbers. Where salvage jobs get posted, news gets shared, and the evening turns into stories.

name: The Boatyard
tagline: Where Sera keeps the fleet afloat
description: Dry docks and repair berths where Solverde's boats are built and maintained. Smells like salt, tar, and sawdust. The sound of tools never fully stops.

name: The Gardens
description: Terraced growing plots climbing the hillside above town. A handful of giant plants tended like old friends. One tomato plant is a tree here. One tomato is a boulder that takes a team to harvest.

name: Tree Cranes
description: Counterweighted cranes built into the trunks of trees along the harbor, used to lift heavy salvage from boats to the upper town. A Maker's pride.

name: The Reef
description: A living reef stretching between the two peninsulas that form the cove. Protects the harbor from open ocean swells. Home to creatures the divers know by name.

name: The Lighthouse
tagline: A human relic still earning its keep
description: A surviving human lighthouse on the eastern peninsula, reactivated and maintained by Solverde. One of the few pieces of giant-scale infrastructure still serving its original purpose.

name: Old Dockyards
tagline: Where the giants kept their ships
description: A sprawling human marina complex outside the cove. Partially collapsed, partially flooded, largely unexplored. The most dangerous and most rewarding salvage site near Solverde. Crews go in prepared and sometimes still come back shaken.

name: Underwater Ruins
description: Submerged human structures visible from the surface on calm days. What's down there is unclear. The divers who've gone deep enough to see don't all agree on what they found.
